title: ScadPub
description: ScadPub publishes OpenSCAD models as static browser-based configurators.

designs.label: Coin
designs.description: Round coin with an embossed emblem.

designs.label: Panel
designs.description: An SVG extruded as a coloured relief panel.

popup.body: Configure it in your browser and export a ready-to-print model — everything runs locally.
popup.footnote: Everything runs in your browser. No file uploads.

help.intro: Customize an OpenSCAD design in your browser and download a ready-to-print model — everything runs on your device.

tabs.label: Walkthrough
tabs.intro: A full pass through the app, start to finish. This tab is intentionally long so you can see it scroll on its own.

sections.title: 1. Pick a design
sections.body: Use the design name in the top bar to switch between the available designs. Each one auto-sizes to the settings you choose.

sections.title: 2. Make it yours
sections.body:
The **Customize** tab lists everything you can change, grouped into sections.

- Drag a slider (or type a number), pick from a dropdown, type text, or flip a switch.
- Tap the small **ⓘ** next to a label for a fuller explanation.
- Click a **section header** to fold or unfold it; some start folded.
- Some settings only appear when another one makes them relevant.
- **Reset to defaults** (at the bottom of the panel) undoes your changes for the current design.

sections.title: 3. Look around the preview
sections.body: **Drag** to spin the model, **scroll or pinch** to zoom, and **right-drag** (or two fingers) to move it.

sections.title: 4. Keep the preview fresh
sections.body: With **Live preview** on, the model updates a moment after each change. For a slow design it pauses itself — press **Update** on the preview when you're ready.

sections.title: 5. Save & reuse settings (presets)
sections.body:
The **Presets** tab lists **Ready-made** starting points and the presets **Saved by you** (stored in this browser).

- **Save** keeps your current settings under a name; **Export** writes them to an OpenSCAD `parameterSets` JSON, which also opens in the desktop OpenSCAD Customizer.

sections.title: 6. Add files (fonts, SVGs…)
sections.body:
Some designs can use a file of yours — a font for lettering, an SVG to `import()`, or a data file for `surface()`. Use **Import file** in the **Files** tab to supply one:

- **Fonts** (`.ttf`/`.otf`) become available to the design's font setting — pick them by the font's family name.
- **Any other file** is used by its name in the design, e.g. `import("logo.svg")`.
- Files are stored in this browser and re-applied on your next visit; the list shows what's loaded.
- **Clear** removes them all. Importing or clearing files rebuilds the preview with the new set.
- Nothing is uploaded to a server — files stay on your device.

sections.title: 7. Download & share
sections.body:
- **Download 3MF** — get the printable mesh (with colours) for your slicer.
- **Image** — save a picture of the preview.
- **Share** — copy a link that reopens this exact design, settings and preset.

sections.title: 8. Appearance & info
sections.body: The **sun/moon** button (top-right) cycles light → dark → auto. The **ⓘ** button lists open-source licenses.

tabs.label: Printing
tabs.intro: Getting a clean print off the downloaded mesh.

sections.title: Material
sections.body: **PLA** prints these designs well. Slice the downloaded 3MF as you would any other mesh.

sections.title: Supports
sections.body: Most designs need none. Add supports only if a part overhangs more than your printer handles.

sections.title: The preview fails or looks wrong
sections.body: Open **Messages** (the bell in the top bar) — the design reports what it didn't like there, and the **Log** tab has the full technical output.
